title: Sophronitis pygmaea
intro: Sophronitis pygmaea, also known as Sophora, is a genus of flowering plants in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sophronitis pygmaea include isoflavones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Sophronitis pygmaea are teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to support immune function and overall health.
taxonomy: Sophronitis pygmaea is now classified as a species within the genus Scaphosepalum, which is part of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). It is a type of terrestrial orchid, originally found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Sophronitis pygmaea was changed due to a reorganization of the genus. This reorganization aimed to better reflect the relationships and characteristics of the orchid species. The revised classification is in line with modern taxonomic practices in the field of botany.
